segments.text: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Jews
And Paul, as his manner was, went in unto them
three sabbath days reasoned with them out of the scriptures
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Opening and alleging, that Christ must needs have suffered, and risen again from the dead; and that this Jesus, whom I preach unto you, is Christ
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But the Jews which believed not, moved with envy, took unto them certain lewd fellows of the baser sort, and gathered a company, and set all the city on an uproar, and assaulted the house of Jason, and sought to bring them out to the people
Then Paul stood in the midst of Mars' hill
Ye men of Athens, I perceive that in all things ye are too superstitious
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For as I passed by, and beheld your devotions, I found an altar with this inscription, TO THE UNKNOWN GOD. Whom therefore ye ignorantly worship, him declare I unto you
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: God that made the world and all things therein, seeing that he is Lord of heaven and earth, dwelleth not in temples made with hands
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And the times of this ignorance God winked at; but now commandeth all men every where to repent
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Howbeit certain men clave unto him, and believed: among the which was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them
